entries.title: GPS jamming and spoofing can degrade tactical PNT
entries.summary: Open-source assessments describe widespread GPS jamming and spoofing around conflict zones, including effects on aircraft, ships, drones, and precision-guided systems.
entries.tactical_relevance: A brigade relying on GPS-enabled drones, fires, blue-force tracking, and timing needs a fast way to detect PNT confidence collapse and switch to fallback posture.
decision_implications: Recommend alternate navigation mode or multi-constellation fallback.
Increase confidence only when PNT anomalies correlate with RF or cyber indicators.

source_refs.locator: GPS Jamming and Spoofing featured analysis
source_refs.claim_supported: GPS interference is widespread and persistent near conflict regions.
Russia fields multiple tactical systems capable of local GPS receiver jamming.

entries.notes: Use as attribution support, not proof of actor intent.

entries.title: SATCOM interference can target ground terminals and uplinks
entries.summary: Public counterspace reporting describes Russian systems and operational patterns for jamming satellite communications, including effects against commercial and broadcast satellites.
entries.tactical_relevance: Brigade beyond-line-of-sight communications can degrade during a multi-domain campaign even when the satellite itself remains intact.
decision_implications: Correlate SATCOM retransmits, link-margin drops, and RF spikes.
Recommend alternate ground route or hardened contact profile.
Escalate when SATCOM degradation overlaps with orbital proximity events.

source_refs.locator: Russian Electronic Warfare / Jamming of Communications Satellites
source_refs.claim_supported: Russia has dedicated capabilities for downlink and uplink jamming of communications satellite signals.

entries.notes: Scenario signals are synthetic operational injects.

entries.title: RPO behavior is dual-use and intent-ambiguous
entries.summary: Rendezvous and proximity operations can support benign inspection or servicing, but the same maneuvering capability can also enable counterspace effects.
entries.tactical_relevance: A commander needs proximity events translated into operational risk without pretending public data proves hostile intent.
decision_implications: Score close approach risk by range, range rate, target dependency, and concurrent multi-domain activity.
Label RPO scenarios as intent-ambiguous unless additional indicators raise confidence.
Draft higher-authority space-effect requests when friendly mission dependency is high.

source_refs.claim_supported: RPO can be hard to distinguish as benign surveillance, servicing, or weapon-like behavior from orbital tracks alone.

source_refs.locator: Chinese Co-Orbital ASAT / Recent Chinese RPOs
source_refs.claim_supported: Chinese RPO demonstrations include SJ-21 relocation of a defunct Beidou satellite and other GEO/LEO proximity activities.

entries.notes: Do not phrase the demo RPO object as a real hostile satellite.

entries.title: Commercial space services are part of the contested battlespace
entries.summary: Commercial imagery, broadband, and communications providers increasingly support military operations and can become targets or sources of operational exposure.
entries.tactical_relevance: The brigade must understand when commercial overpass, imagery tasking, or SATCOM dependency changes ground-force risk.
decision_implications: Surface commercial collection windows as operational risk.
Mark commercial-source claims with provenance and uncertainty.
Recommend masking or timing changes when overpass and ground activity correlate.

source_refs.locator: The Coming Collision of Commercial and Counterspace
source_refs.claim_supported: Commercial space services supporting defense users are increasingly in the crosshairs.

entries.notes: Avoid unsupported claims about a specific commercial provider unless sourced.

entries.title: Public indicators support fast assessment, not definitive attribution
entries.summary: Open-source reporting can support fast, doctrine-grounded hypotheses, but it cannot replace classified all-source attribution or prove adversary intent.
entries.tactical_relevance: CANOPY should give commanders an actionable confidence-scored assessment while preserving humility and escalation discipline.
decision_implications: Use language like 'consistent with' and 'confidence-scored assessment'.
Preserve evidence chains for every attribution.
Fall back to 'insufficient signal' when evidence is thin.

source_refs.locator: Counterspace weapons taxonomy / cyber and electronic effects discussion
source_refs.claim_supported: Attribution varies by attack mode and can be limited or uncertain.

entries.notes: This entry should guide all pitch and UI language.

entries.title: Iranian and Iranian-proxy counter-C5ISR pattern
entries.summary: Iran and Iran-aligned proxies have repeatedly paired GPS spoofing, UAS C2-link emissions, RF interference on tactical frequencies, and credential-access cyber probing to degrade U.S. and partner C5ISR — most prominently around the Strait of Hormuz, Iraq, Syria, and base-defense layers in CENTCOM.
entries.tactical_relevance: When a brigade-level cluster combines GPS spoofing with detected UAS control links and concurrent cyber probing on adjacent infrastructure, the pattern is consistent with Iranian or Iran-proxy tradecraft and should be attributed accordingly with mid-band confidence.
decision_implications: Pair GPS spoofing with UAS C2 detection or maritime denial cues to raise Iran-attribution confidence.
Cyber credential probing on the same window is a corroborating cue, not standalone attribution.
Iran-proxy attributions should default to 'Iran' unless the cluster names a specific proxy group with KB support.

source_refs.locator: Counterspace and counter-PNT activity by regional powers
source_refs.claim_supported: Iran has demonstrated GNSS interference and UAS-enabled denial in maritime and land theaters.
Iran maintains and proliferates electronic warfare and UAS systems used by aligned proxies.

entries.notes: Use 'Iran' rather than 'Iranian proxy' as the headline actor unless a specific proxy group is named with KB support.
